history.hypothesis: Index ETFs like SPY, QQQ, IWM exhibit smoother trends than individual stocks, making them ideal for breakout strategies with volume confirmation. Donchian Channel breakouts filtered by above-average volume and a 50 EMA trend filter should capture major trend initiations while avoiding false breakouts. ATR-based trailing stops will protect profits during volatile reversals, keeping drawdown under 20% while targeting 1.0+ Sharpe.

The strategy is far too selective for index ETFs — requiring simultaneous 20-day Donchian breakout + 1.3x volume spike + 50 EMA trend filter produces only 3 trades in 2 years. Index ETFs like SPY have gradual volume profiles, making volume spike filters too restrictive. Switching to Keltner Channels (EMA + ATR) with a shorter lookback (15 vs 20), removing the volume multiplier requirement (just above average), and using a shorter trend EMA (30 vs 50) will generate 10+ trades while maintaining breakout edge. Exit at channel midpoint (EMA) instead of trailing stop to avoid premature exits on normal pullbacks.
